name: AlbumCoverGenerator
tagline: Make album covers from emojis, gradients, and bold text.
description: A small browser tool that turns emojis, gradients, and one line of bold text into a 3000x3000 album cover you can download as JPEG or PNG. Pick two colors, choose linear or radial, drop in any emoji palette, set rotation and opacity, add a title and artist, hit Download. No login, no signup, no AI generation. It won't replace a real designer; it'll replace 20 minutes of fiddling with stock templates when you just need a cover for a playlist or one-off mix.
first_comment: Hey Hunters! I'm Moises. I built AlbumCoverGenerator because the default Spotify placeholder bothers me and Photoshop is overkill for a one-off mix. The whole tool runs in your browser. Pick two colors, choose linear or radial gradient, drop in any emoji palette, tune count and rotation and opacity, add a title and artist, pick a font, hit Download. There's a Shuffle Everything button if you want the dice to roll for you, and a 200ms debounce on regenerate so dragging sliders stays smooth. Output is a 3000x3000 PNG or JPEG with a 20MB guard. No login, no signup, no AI generation, so it won't hallucinate weird hands or extra fingers, but it also won't surprise you with a brilliant idea. It will spit out a clean, deterministic cover in about 30 seconds. This is the v1. The roadmap is open: I'm thinking pattern overlays, pixel-art mode, maybe importing your own SVG icons instead of emojis. What kind of cover do you wish existed? Drop a comment with the rough idea and I'll prioritize the next build around it.
